memory.summary: The leisure option feels tempting given my low stress, but the term project deadline looms too close to ignore. I need to prioritize the quick chore first before any relaxation.

desires: Secure the remaining project milestone before my energy completely collapses so I don't have to explain a catastrophic failure to Professor Assignment.

goals.description: Execute a final, uninterrupted 90-minute deep-work block on the Term Project to bridge the gap from 81% to ~90%, ensuring no further friction with Professor prof_assignment and strictly avoiding any engagement with the 'quick_chore' or social validation from cohort_3.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive demands I secure high completion rates by Thursday to mitigate stress. The insight that every hour now saves 1.5 hours later confirms that procrastination compounds my anxiety. Continuing to chase 'instant wins' on the trivial chore violates my long planning horizon and risks eroding the focus capacity needed for this high-value academic task. I will suppress the urge to explain my exhaustion to cohort_3; preserving face is secondary to delivering the work.
